name: Marseilles Bank
description_short: Marseilles Bank is a community bank in Illinois offering personal and business banking services, loans, deposit accounts, and digital banking tools.
description_long:
Marseilles Bank is a full-service community bank headquartered in Marseilles, Illinois (LaSalle County), operating under NMLS# 835208. The bank has positioned itself as "The Extraordinary Bank," emphasizing personalized customer service alongside competitive loan and deposit rates. The institution maintains a physical branch at 100 E. Bluff Street and offers both in-person and digital banking channels.

The bank provides a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. On the deposit side, they offer checking accounts with imaged statements, e-statements, and mobile banking with mobile deposit capabilities. Loan products are available through three named mortgage loan originators (Jackie Jo Oelschlager, Matt Mooneyham, and Ashley Marie Ziel), and the bank advertises competitive loan rates and financial calculators for payment projections. They also issue a co-branded MasterCard credit card and provide traditional banking services.

Marseilles Bank distinguishes itself through direct local leadership and board involvement, with the President (Matthew D. Mooneyham) and board members actively listed on their website. The bank accepts LaSalle County real estate tax payments, suggesting deep community integration. They offer both standard banking conveniences (safe deposit boxes, notary service, wire transfers, cashier's checks) and modern digital features (mobile app downloads, 24-hour telephone banking, MasterCard Secure Code protection).

As a regional community bank, Marseilles Bank serves local customers who prioritize relationship banking and community connection over national brand recognition. However, the website provides limited detail on specific account features, APY rates, loan terms, fees, or minimum balance requirements. The bank's single physical location may limit accessibility for customers outside the immediate area, and product specificity is less detailed than larger national institutions.
services: Checking accounts with imaged monthly statements
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit
ATM and MasterCard EMV debit cards
MasterCard Secure Code protection for online shopping
Mortgage lending through licensed loan originators
Financial calculators for loans and investments
pros: Offers mobile banking with mobile deposit capability for remote check deposits
Provides 24-hour telephone banking for account access and service outside business hours
Issues ATM and MasterCard EMV debit cards with Secure Code protection for online security
Accepts LaSalle County real estate tax payments, integrating local government services
Has three named, NMLS-licensed mortgage loan originators on staff
Offers financial calculators on website for loan payment and investment projections
Provides safe deposit boxes and notary services in-house
cons: Single branch location in Marseilles, IL limits accessibility for non-local customers
Website lacks specific details on APY rates, account fees, minimum balances, or loan terms
No information about deposit insurance coverage (FDIC) explicitly stated on provided pages
Limited product descriptions; business banking and personal banking sections appear on navigation but detailed offerings not provided in website content
No mention of online account opening or whether accounts can be opened remotely
best_for: LaSalle County residents seeking local relationship banking with direct board/officer access
Small business owners in Illinois needing community bank support and real estate tax payment services
Customers prioritizing personalized service and local decision-making over national brand banking
Borrowers seeking mortgage financing from named local loan officers with NMLS credentials
diagnosis: Marseilles Bank is best for individuals and small businesses in LaSalle County, Illinois, seeking personal relationship banking with local decision-makers and competitive rates. The main caveat is that as a single-branch community bank, it offers limited accessibility for remote or out-of-area customers, and the website provides insufficient detail on specific rates, fees, and product terms to enable informed comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
